title: Container Sandbox Sessions (Docker / Modal / E2B)
interaction_flow: DockerSandboxClient → DockerSandboxSession: new DockerSandboxSession(docker_client, container, state)
DockerSandboxSession → BaseSandboxSession: _start_workspace()  [snapshot restore OR manifest apply]
Caller → DockerSandboxSession: exec(*command, timeout, shell, user)
DockerSandboxSession → DockerSDK: docker API exec_create + exec_start (socket-based)
DockerSandboxSession → Caller: return ExecResult(stdout, stderr, exit_code)
Caller → ModalSandboxClient: create(manifest, options[app_name, image, timeout, workspace_persistence])
ModalSandboxSession → BaseSandboxSession: _start_workspace()  [snapshot restore OR manifest apply]
Caller → ModalSandboxSession: exec(*command, timeout, shell, user)
ModalSandboxSession → Caller: return ExecResult(stdout, stderr, exit_code)
ModalSandboxSession → ModalSDK: sandbox.snapshot_filesystem() or snapshot via tar exec
Caller → E2BSandboxClient: create(manifest, options[template, timeout, sandbox_type, workspace_persistence])
E2BSandboxClient → E2BSDK: _sandbox_create(SandboxClass, template, timeout, metadata, envs)
E2BSandboxSession → BaseSandboxSession: _start_workspace()  [snapshot restore OR manifest apply]
E2BSandboxSession → E2BSandboxSession: _after_start()  [reinstalls runtime helpers if cache key changed]
Caller → E2BSandboxSession: exec(*command, timeout, shell, user)
E2BSandboxSession → E2BSDK: _sandbox_run_command(sandbox, cmd, timeout, cwd, envs, user)
E2BSandboxSession → Caller: return ExecResult(stdout, stderr, exit_code)
E2BSandboxSession → E2BSDK: _sandbox_create_snapshot(sandbox) OR tar exec + read

key_methods.BaseSandboxSession._start_workspace: If restorable snapshot: restore and reapply ephemeral. If preserved workspace: reapply ephemeral. Else: full _apply_manifest()
key_methods.DockerSandboxSession._ensure_backend_sta: container.reload() then container.start() if not running
key_methods.DockerSandboxSession._exec_run: docker exec_create + exec_start via socket; streams stdout/stderr via _DockerExecSocket
key_methods.ModalSandboxSession._call_modal: Prefers fn.aio(*args); falls back to loop.run_in_executor(fn, args) with optional asyncio.wait_for timeout
key_methods.ModalSandboxSession._ensure_sandbox: If sandbox_id exists: from_id → poll; if alive reuse, else clear. Then: App.lookup(create_if_missing=True) → modal.Sandbox.create()
key_methods.E2BSandboxSession._after_start: Reinstalls runtime helpers if backend sandbox_id changed

key_relationships: ModalSandboxClient creates ModalSandboxSession via from_state; calls _ensure_sandbox eagerly
E2BSandboxClient creates E2BSandboxSession after _sandbox_create; sandbox already live
All sessions extend BaseSandboxSession (template method pattern for lifecycle hooks)
ModalSandboxSession wraps all Modal SDK calls via _call_modal (async adapter with fallback)
E2BSandboxSession wraps E2B SDK via module-level helpers (_sandbox_create, _sandbox_kill, _sandbox_pause, _sandbox_run_command)
DockerSandboxSession uses docker.Container directly and DockerSDKClient for socket-level exec
important_notes: E2B sandbox is created upfront in create(); no _ensure_backend_started needed (sandbox already live)
Modal sandbox is also created in create() via _ensure_sandbox(); resume path tries from_id + poll
Docker _ensure_backend_started reloads and starts a stopped container (resume scenario)
stop() is persistence-only (snapshot/tar); shutdown() tears down container/sandbox resources
Modal _call_modal prefers .aio on every SDK method; fallback ensures thread-safety
E2B SDK imports are lazy (_import_sandbox_class) to keep the package optional
pause_on_exit flag on E2B controls whether shutdown pauses (restorable) or kills the sandbox
ModalSandboxSession._prepare_backend_workspace always runs mkdir -p on workspace root before manifest apply
All backends support pty_exec_start / pty_write_stdin / pty_terminate_all for interactive shells
